1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

## Python CLI Bookmarks Project - Complete Exploration Report

### 1. Directory Structure

```
.
├── pyproject.toml                    # Project configuration

├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py                   # Package init (empty)

│   ├── __init__.py                   # Test package init (empty)
│   ├── test_storage.py               # Storage layer tests
│   └── test_search.py                # Search feature tests (defines expected API)
```

### 2. Project Configuration (pyproject.toml)

**Project Details:**
- Name: `bookmark-cli`
- Version: `1.0.0`
- Description: A CLI tool for managing a local bookmark collection
- Requires Python: >=3.10
- Entry point: `bookmark = "src.cli:main"`

**Dependencies:**
- No runtime dependencies (uses only standard library)
- Test dependencies: `pytest>=7.4.0`

### 3. Data Model (src/models.py)

**Bookmark Dataclass** - The core entity:

```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str                    # UUID string (generated on creation)
    url: str                   # The bookmarked URL
    title: str                 # Display title
    tags: List[str]           # Optional tags (default: [])
    notes: str                # Optional notes (default: "")
    created_at: str           # ISO format datetime (auto-set to now)
```

**Key Methods:**
- `to_dict()` → Returns dict representation for JSON serialization
- `from_dict(data)` → Class method to reconstruct from dict

**Storage Format:** Bookmarks are persisted as JSON at `~/.bookmarks.json`

### 4. Storage Layer (src/storage.py)

**Database Path:** `~/.bookmarks.json` (default, configurable)

**Core Functions:**

1. **`load_bookmarks(db_path)`** → `List[Bookmark]`
   - Loads all bookmarks from JSON file
   - Returns empty list if file doesn't exist

2. **`save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)`** → `None`
   - Writes all bookmarks to JSON file with 2-space indentation

3. **`add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path)`** → `Bookmark`
   - Creates new bookmark with UUID
   - Persists to storage
   - Returns the created bookmark

4. **`delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** → `bool`
   - Returns True if deleted, False if ID not found

5. **`get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** → `Optional[Bookmark]`
   - Returns single bookmark by ID or None

**Pattern:** All functions support custom `db_path` parameter for testing

### 5. CLI Structure (src/cli.py)

**Command Structure:**

```
bookmark <command> [options]
```

**Available Commands:**

1. **`add`** - Add a new bookmark
   - Arguments: `url` (positional), `title` (positional)
   - Options: `--tags` (zero or more), `--notes` (optional)
   - Output: "Bookmark added: {id}"

2. **`list`** - List all bookmarks
   - Options: `--format` (choices: "table" or "json", default: "table")
   - Output: Formatted list of all bookmarks

3. **`delete`** - Delete a bookmark
   - Arguments: `id` (positional)
   - Output: Success/failure message, exits with code 1 on failure

4. **`show`** - Display single bookmark
   - Arguments: `id` (positional)
   - Output: Single bookmark in table format, exits with code 1 if not found

**CLI Architecture:**
- Uses `argparse.ArgumentParser` with subparsers
- `build_parser()` constructs and returns parser
- `main(argv=None)` entry point parses args and dispatches to handler functions
- Default behavior: prints help and exits with code 1 if no command given

### 6. Output Formatting (src/formatter.py)

**`format_table(bookmarks)`** → `str`
- Creates plain-text table with columns: ID, Title, URL, Tags
- Column widths: ID (8), Title (30), URL (40), Tags (20)
- Truncates content to fit column widths
- Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty list

**`format_json(bookmarks)`** → `str`
- Outputs JSON array with 2-space indentation
- Uses `Bookmark.to_dict()` for serialization

### 7. Test Coverage

**test_storage.py** - 13 tests covering:
- Loading bookmarks (missing file, valid file)
- Saving bookmarks (creation, valid JSON)
- Adding bookmarks (creation, persistence, unique IDs)
- Deleting bookmarks (existing, missing ID)
- Getting bookmarks (by ID, missing ID)

**test_search.py** - Expected behavior for UNIMPLEMENTED features:
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` - Case-insensitive substring match
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` - Exact tag matching
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` - URL domain extraction and matching (including subdomains)
- `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)` - Combined search with AND logic
- CLI: `search` command with `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, and `--format` flags

### 8. Key Patterns to Follow

**Storage Pattern:**
- All storage functions accept optional `db_path` parameter
- Load → Modify → Save pattern for mutations
- UUID generation for new bookmarks

**CLI Pattern:**
- Subparser for each command
- Exit code 1 on errors (invalid ID, not found)
- Print user-friendly messages

**Output Pattern:**
- Multiple formatter functions for different output types
- Both table and JSON formats supported

**Data Pattern:**
- Use dataclass with `field(default_factory=...)` for mutable defaults
- `to_dict()`/`from_dict()` for serialization

**Naming Convention:**
- Snake_case for functions
- Positional args for required parameters
- Optional flags with `--` prefix

### 9. Missing Implementation (test_search.py)

The test file expects a **`src/search.py`** module with:

1. **`search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Case-insensitive substring matching in title field

2. **`search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Exact matching against tags list

3. **`search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Extract domain from URL and match (including subdomains like `docs.python.org`)

4. **`search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Combined search with AND logic (all provided criteria must match)
   - Returns all bookmarks if no criteria provided

And CLI integration in `src/cli.py`:
- New `search` subcommand with `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain` options
- `--format` flag for output (table/json)
- Main function dispatch for search command

This is the feature that needs implementation based on the test expectations.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmarks CLI can add, list, delete, and show bookmarks but has no way to filter or find them. This adds a `search` command backed by a new `src/search.py` module. The test expectations are already fully specified in `tests/test_search.py`, so the implementation must satisfy those exactly.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `src/search.py` |
| **Modify** | `src/cli.py` |

No other files need changes.

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib, no new deps) to extract the domain/netloc from URLs.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match by URL domain (netloc). e.g. 'docs.python.org' or 'github.com'."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combined search with AND logic. No criteria → returns all bookmarks."""
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

**Domain matching note:** The tests use exact `netloc` comparison (`docs.python.org == docs.python.org`), so `urlparse(url).netloc` is sufficient — no subdomain-stripping needed.

---

## Step 2 — Modify `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import at the top

Add after the existing imports:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add `search` subparser inside `build_parser()`

Add after the `show` subparser block (before `return parser`):
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag",   default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Add handler in `main()`

Add after the `elif args.command == "show":` block:
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite — all existing tests plus the new search tests must pass:

```bash
pytest tests/
```

Expected: all tests in `tests/test_storage.py` continue to pass; all tests in `tests/test_search.py` now pass (covers `TestSearchByTitle`, `TestSearchByTag`, `TestSearchByDomain`, `TestSearchCombined`, `TestSearchCLI`).

Manual smoke-test:
```bash

bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --domain docs.python.org
bookmark search --title python --tag docs --domain docs.python.org
```

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib, no new deps) to extract the domain/netloc from URLs.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match by URL domain (netloc). e.g. 'docs.python.org' or 'github.com'."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combined search with AND logic. No criteria → returns all bookmarks."""
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

**Domain matching note:** The tests use exact `netloc` comparison (`docs.python.org == docs.python.org`), so `urlparse(url).netloc` is sufficient — no subdomain-stripping needed.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json
from .search import search


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title (substring)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by URL domain")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
